prompt: 14th-century Gothic manuscript illumination on aged vellum depicting a 'Random Forest' ensemble. Multiple branching 'Trees of Decision' (Arbores Decisionis) intertwined with acanthus leaves. Nodes are burnished gold leaf circles containing Latinized data labels in Gothic blackletter script ('Si Aetas < XXX'). Roots emerge from a 'Data Radix' base. Flat spatial organization, no linear perspective, tempera palette of vermilion, lapis lazuli, and malachite. Decorative marginalia featuring a rabbit and snail. High-resolution authentic parchment texture, heavy ink outlines.
concept: Random Forest / Ensemble of Decision Trees
tradition_approach: Applied the 'Arbor Virtutum' hierarchical branching logic to visualize decision nodes. Utilized flat spatial organization, burnished gold leaf for leaf-nodes, and 'littera textualis' typography to avoid anachronistic modern diagrammatic conventions while maintaining binary-split discernibility.
